# Selfchecks

Selfchecks is a self-hosted synthetic checks runner and dashboard for projects
that already describe monitoring as code.

Canonical site: https://selfchecks.github.io/
Getting started: https://selfchecks.github.io/getting-started.html
Main source: https://github.com/selfchecks/selfchecks

## Documentation

The Getting Started guide is the canonical external documentation for creating
a project, configuring Playwright and Checkly-compatible manifests, writing API
and browser checks, selecting test modules, using the authenticated HTTP API,
setting up GitLab CI/CD and GitHub Actions, and deploying a Selfchecks server.

## npm packages

- `create-selfchecks`: project generator, run as `npx create-selfchecks`.
- `@selfchecks/selfchecks-cli`: remote `selfchecks` CLI for deploy, test, and trigger.
- `@selfchecks/selfchecks`: supported checks-as-code constructs.

The fastest start is `npx create-selfchecks my-checks`. It creates and installs a
minimal project with one Playwright browser check for
`https://selfchecks.github.io/`.

Projects migrating from Checkly can keep their existing import specifiers with
the npm alias `"checkly": "npm:@selfchecks/selfchecks@0.1.0"`.

## Positioning

Selfchecks keeps a repository-owned checks-as-code workflow while replacing a
hosted control plane with a small self-hosted service. It targets API checks,
browser checks, grouped checks, tags, schedules, artifacts, CI reports, and
webhook notifications. Checkly-style compatibility is an import and migration
feature, not the core identity of the product.

## Useful commands

- `selfchecks deploy --force`
- `selfchecks test --reporter=github --record`
- `selfchecks trigger --reporter=github --retries=1 --record`

## Checkly migration subset

Supported from `checkly`: `defineConfig`.

Supported from `checkly/constructs`: `ApiCheck`, `BrowserCheck`, `CheckGroup`,
`CheckGroupV2`, the documented `Frequency` constants, `AssertionBuilder`,
`RetryStrategyBuilder`, `AlertEscalationBuilder.runBasedEscalation`,
`WebhookAlertChannel`, and the related props/request types. Compatibility is
intentionally narrow. Assertion builder expressions, alert channels, and
escalation objects provide source compatibility but are not deployed by the
current importer. Other Checkly APIs and runtime behavior are unsupported.

## Remote CI flow

Set `SELFCHECKS_URL` to the dashboard URL and `SELFCHECKS_API_TOKEN` to a valid
project API token. The CLI uploads deploy and test bundles through the
authenticated HTTP API. `selfchecks trigger` queues checks from the latest
successful deployment. Project CI does not require SSH or rsync credentials.

Recorded test sessions store CI metadata as separate fields rather than a
delimiter-based source string:

- `repository` (`--repository`, defaults to `CI_PROJECT_PATH`)
- `ref` (`--ref`, defaults to the GitLab branch or tag)
- `commitSha` (`--commit-sha`, defaults to `CI_COMMIT_SHA`)
- `pipelineUrl` (`--pipeline-url`, defaults to `CI_PIPELINE_URL`)
- `jobUrl` (`--job-url`, defaults to `CI_JOB_URL`)

## MVP stack

- Next.js dashboard
- PostgreSQL database
- BullMQ and Redis worker queue
- Playwright browser runner
- native fetch / Undici API runner
- generic webhooks with Rocket.Chat as the first concrete adapter
